commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `errorBody` to Anthropic thinking-recovery error traversal and adds a focused regression case for a ProviderHttpError-like error body.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +12. Total +13 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `shouldRecoverAnthropicThinkingError` does not traverse `errorBody`, while `ProviderHttpError` stores bounded provider response body text there.

target.title: fix(msteams): escape markup in mention display names
target.body:
**Summary:** `parseMentions` embedded display names inside `<at>…</at>` tags without HTML-escaping, so a name containing `<`, `>`, or `&` corrupted the Teams mention entity payload.

## What Problem This Solves

The Teams adapter builds mention entities by interpolating the raw display name into `<at>{name}</at>`. Microsoft Teams parses `activity.text` as XML markup to extract `<at>` mentions; it also requires `entity.text` to be a literal substring of `activity.text`. A display name with HTML-special characters breaks both requirements:

- `Alice <Admin> & Bob` → `<at>Alice <Admin> & Bob</at>` — Teams XML parser sees `<Admin>` as a child element inside `<at>`, corrupting the mention structure
- `<at>Eve</at>` → `<at><at>Eve</at></at>` — nested `<at>` injection creates two mention elements

## Fix

Added `escapeTeamsMentionText(value)` that replaces `&` → `&`, `<` → `<`, `>` → `>` before wrapping in `<at>…</at>`. The `mentioned.name` field is left unescaped so the display name stored in the entity record remains human-readable.

## Evidence

### 1. Full `buildActivity` call chain driven via `node --import tsx`

The proof drives `buildActivity → parseMentions` (the real exported function, text-only path) and parses `activity.text` with **jsdom** — an independent HTML5 DOM parser — to assert structural correctness without relying on the code under test for verification.

**BEFORE (pre-fix: raw name interpolated into `<at>…</at>`):**

```
[✗ BROKEN] angle brackets + ampersand: Alice <Admin> & Bob
  tag:         <at>Alice <Admin> & Bob</at>
  <at> count:  1  nested: 0  injected child: true   ← <Admin> parsed as DOM child
  textContent: Alice  & Bob                          ← name truncated/corrupted

[✗ BROKEN] nested <at> injection: <at>Eve</at>
  tag:         <at><at>Eve</at></at>
  <at> count:  2  nested: 1  injected child: true   ← two <at> elements, nested
  textContent: Eve

[✓] clean name (no-op): Normal Name
  tag:         <at>Normal Name</at>
  <at> count:  1  nested: 0  injected child: false
```

**AFTER (PR branch — `escapeTeamsMentionText` applied, sha 4a6296138e):**

```
[✓] angle brackets + ampersand: Alice <Admin> & Bob
  activity.text:   <at>Alice <Admin> & Bob</at>
  entity.text:     <at>Alice <Admin> & Bob</at>
  entity.name:     Alice <Admin> & Bob
  <at> count: 1  nested: 0  injected child: false
  entity aligned: true  name preserved: true

[✓] nested <at> injection: <at>Eve</at>
  activity.text:   <at><at>Eve</at></at>
  entity.text:     <at><at>Eve</at></at>
  entity.name:     <at>Eve</at>
  <at> count: 1  nested: 0  injected child: false
  entity aligned: true  name preserved: true

[✓] clean name (no-op): Normal Name
  activity.text:   <at>Normal Name</at>
  entity.text:     <at>Normal Name</at>
  entity.name:     Normal Name
  <at> count: 1  nested: 0  injected child: false
  entity aligned: true  name preserved: true
```

### 2. Vitest run — `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/msteams/src/mentions.test.ts`

```
RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
   Duration  14.69s
```

## Tests

18 tests pass across three describe blocks (15 existing + 3 new `buildActivity mention markup — jsdom structural oracle`):

**New jsdom-backed structural tests (independent oracle, not the code under test):**

- `produces exactly one well-formed <at> for a name with angle brackets and ampersand` — drives `buildActivity({ text: "@[Alice <Admin> & Bob](28:abc-00001)" }, {})`, asserts `atCount=1`, `nestedAt=0`, `hasInjectedChild=false`, `entity.text` is a literal substring of `activity.text`
- `produces exactly one <at> and blocks nested <at> injection` — drives `@[<at>Eve</at>](28:abc-00002)`, asserts no nested `<at>` in DOM, raw name preserved in `entity.mentioned.name`
- **Negative control** — confirms pre-fix raw interpolation (`<at>Alice <Admin> & Bob</at>`) produces `hasInjectedChild=true` and truncated `textContent`; nested injection produces `nestedAt > 0`

**Existing parser-level tests (15):**
- `escapes markup characters in mention tags and keeps entity text aligned`
- `escapes closing at tags inside mention names`
- Plus 13 other coverage tests
